# Example 1: Simple Linear Process (VALID)
# Demonstrates: Basic process structure with user and service tasks
# Rules followed: ONE_START_EVENT, AT_LEAST_ONE_END_EVENT, ALL_OBJECTS_CONNECTED
from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, SequenceFlow

p = Process("Simple Approval Process")
start = StartEvent("start", "Start")
t1 = Task("task1", "Submit Request", "user")
t2 = Task("task2", "Validate Request", "service")
t3 = Task("task3", "Review Application", "user")
end = EndEvent("end", "End")

p.add(start)
p.add(t1)
p.add(t2)
p.add(t3)
p.add(end)

p.add(SequenceFlow(start, t1))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t1, t2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t2, t3))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t3, end))
', '
# Example 2: Exclusive Gateway (XOR) - Decision Process (VALID)
# Demonstrates: Branching and merging with exclusive gateway
# Rules followed: GATEWAY_BRANCHING_RULE, PATH_SYMMETRY_RULE, EXCLUSIVE_GATEWAY_BEHAVIOR
from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

p = Process("Loan Approval Process")
start = StartEvent("start", "Start")
t1 = Task("task1", "Submit Application", "user")
t2 = Task("task2", "Check Credit Score", "service")
gw1 = Gateway("gw1", "Credit Decision", "exclusive")  # Splitting gateway
t3 = Task("task3", "Approve Loan", "user")
t4 = Task("task4", "Reject Application", "user")
gw2 = Gateway("gw2", "Merge", "exclusive")  # Joining gateway
t5 = Task("task5", "Notify Customer", "service")
end = EndEvent("end", "End")

p.add(start)
p.add(t1)
p.add(t2)
p.add(gw1)
p.add(t3)
p.add(t4)
p.add(gw2)
p.add(t5)
p.add(end)

# Main flow
p.add(SequenceFlow(start, t1))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t1, t2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t2, gw1))
# Branching paths
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t3))  # Good credit path
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t4))  # Bad credit path
# Merging paths
p.add(SequenceFlow(t3, gw2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t4, gw2))
# Continue after merge
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw2, t5))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t5, end))
', '
# Example 3: Parallel Gateway (AND) - Concurrent Execution (VALID)
# Demonstrates: Parallel execution of tasks that must all complete
# Rules followed: PARALLEL_GATEWAY_BEHAVIOR, PATH_SYMMETRY_RULE
from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

p = Process("Order Fulfillment Process")
start = StartEvent("start", "Start")
t1 = Task("task1", "Receive Order", "user")
gw1 = Gateway("gw1", "Parallel Split", "parallel")  # Splitting gateway
t2 = Task("task2", "Prepare Shipment", "service")
t3 = Task("task3", "Generate Invoice", "service")
t4 = Task("task4", "Update Inventory", "service")
gw2 = Gateway("gw2", "Parallel Join", "parallel")  # Joining gateway
t5 = Task("task5", "Send Notification", "user")
end = EndEvent("end", "End")

p.add(start)
p.add(t1)
p.add(gw1)
p.add(t2)
p.add(t3)
p.add(t4)
p.add(gw2)
p.add(t5)
p.add(end)

# Main flow
p.add(SequenceFlow(start, t1))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t1, gw1))
# Three parallel paths
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t3))
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t4))
# All paths converge
p.add(SequenceFlow(t2, gw2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t3, gw2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t4, gw2))
# Continue after join
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw2, t5))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t5, end))
', '
# Example 4: Script Task with Error Handling (VALID)
# Demonstrates: Mandatory error handling for script tasks
# Rules followed: MANDATORY_ERROR_HANDLING_FOR_SCRIPTS
from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

p = Process("Data Processing Workflow")
start = StartEvent("start", "Start")
t1 = Task("task1", "Load Data", "user")
t2 = Task("task2", "Transform Data", "script")  # Script task
gw1 = Gateway("gw1", "Check Result", "exclusive")  # Error handling gateway (required!)
t3 = Task("task3", "Save Results", "service")
t4 = Task("task4", "Log Error", "user")
gw2 = Gateway("gw2", "Merge", "exclusive")
end = EndEvent("end", "End")

p.add(start)
p.add(t1)
p.add(t2)
p.add(gw1)
p.add(t3)
p.add(t4)
p.add(gw2)
p.add(end)

p.add(SequenceFlow(start, t1))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t1, t2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t2, gw1))  # Script task MUST be followed by gateway
# Error handling paths
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t3))  # Success path
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t4))  # Error path
# Merge and end
p.add(SequenceFlow(t3, gw2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t4, gw2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw2, end))
', '
# Example 5: Complex Multi-Gateway Process (VALID)
# Demonstrates: Multiple decision points with proper path convergence
from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

p = Process("Expense Approval Process")
start = StartEvent("start", "Start")
t1 = Task("task1", "Submit Expense", "user")
gw1 = Gateway("gw1", "Amount Check", "exclusive")
t2 = Task("task2", "Auto Approve", "service")
t3 = Task("task3", "Manager Review", "user")
gw2 = Gateway("gw2", "Manager Decision", "exclusive")
t4 = Task("task4", "Approve", "user")
t5 = Task("task5", "Reject", "user")
gw3 = Gateway("gw3", "Merge All", "exclusive")  # Merges all paths
t6 = Task("task6", "Notify Employee", "service")
end = EndEvent("end", "End")

p.add(start)
p.add(t1)
p.add(gw1)
p.add(t2)
p.add(t3)
p.add(gw2)
p.add(t4)
p.add(t5)
p.add(gw3)
p.add(t6)
p.add(end)

p.add(SequenceFlow(start, t1))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t1, gw1))
# First branch: amount-based
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t2))  # < $100
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw1, t3))  # >= $100
# Second branch: manager decision
p.add(SequenceFlow(t3, gw2))
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw2, t4))  # Approved
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw2, t5))  # Rejected
# Merge all paths
p.add(SequenceFlow(t2, gw3))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t4, gw3))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t5, gw3))
# Final notification
p.add(SequenceFlow(gw3, t6))
p.add(SequenceFlow(t6, end))
', '

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

p.add(start)

p.add(gw1)
p.add(t2)
p.add(t3)
p.add(t4)
p.add(gw2)
p.add(t5)
p.add(end)

p.add(SequenceFlow(start, t1))

You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. Tasks must use valid BPMN 2.0 task types: abstract (generic task), user (human-performed), service (automated service), manual (physical task outside system), script (script execution), send (message sending), receive (message receiving), business_rule (business rules execution). A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event.

results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. Gateways must use valid BPMN 2.0 gateway types: exclusive (XOR - only one path), parallel (AND - all paths simultaneously), inclusive (OR - one or more paths), event_based (decision based on events), complex (complex decision logic). A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event.

Create a loan process: start → submit application → exclusive gateway checks credit → if good: parallel gateway (process docs AND verify income) → join → approve → end; if bad: reject → end

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Loan Approval Process')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
submit_application = Task('task_submit', 'submit application', 'user')

# Exclusive decision: credit check (split)
gw_credit = Gateway('gw_credit', 'Credit Check', 'exclusive')

# Good-credit branch: parallel execution of two service tasks
gw_parallel_split = Gateway('gw_parallel_split', 'Parallel Split', 'parallel')
process_docs = Task('task_process_docs', 'process docs', 'service')
verify_income = Task('task_verify_income', 'verify income', 'service')
gw_parallel_join = Gateway('gw_parallel_join', 'Parallel Join', 'parallel')

approve = Task('task_approve', 'approve', 'user')

# Bad-credit branch
reject = Task('task_reject', 'reject', 'user')

# Join for the exclusive decision (merge approve/reject)
gw_credit_join = Gateway('gw_credit_join', 'Credit Join', 'exclusive')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(submit_application)
process.add(gw_credit)

process.add(gw_parallel_split)
process.add(process_docs)
process.add(verify_income)
process.add(gw_parallel_join)

process.add(approve)
process.add(reject)

process.add(gw_credit_join)
process.add(end)

# Sequence flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, submit_application))
process.add(SequenceFlow(submit_application, gw_credit))

# Credit check branching
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_credit, gw_parallel_split))  # good credit path -> parallel split
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_credit, reject))             # bad credit path -> reject

# Parallel paths for good credit
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, process_docs))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, verify_income))

# Join parallel paths and continue to approval
process.add(SequenceFlow(process_docs, gw_parallel_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(verify_income, gw_parallel_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_join, approve))

# Merge approve and reject to a single end
process.add(SequenceFlow(approve, gw_credit_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(reject, gw_credit_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_credit_join, end))

args.code:
from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Loan Approval Process')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
submit_application = Task('task_submit', 'submit application', 'user')

# Exclusive decision: credit check (split)
gw_credit = Gateway('gw_credit', 'Credit Check', 'exclusive')

# Good-credit branch: parallel execution of two service tasks
gw_parallel_split = Gateway('gw_parallel_split', 'Parallel Split', 'parallel')
process_docs = Task('task_process_docs', 'process docs', 'service')
verify_income = Task('task_verify_income', 'verify income', 'service')
gw_parallel_join = Gateway('gw_parallel_join', 'Parallel Join', 'parallel')

approve = Task('task_approve', 'approve', 'user')

# Bad-credit branch
reject = Task('task_reject', 'reject', 'user')

# Join for the exclusive decision (merge approve/reject)
gw_credit_join = Gateway('gw_credit_join', 'Credit Join', 'exclusive')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(submit_application)
process.add(gw_credit)

process.add(gw_parallel_split)
process.add(process_docs)
process.add(verify_income)
process.add(gw_parallel_join)

process.add(approve)
process.add(reject)

process.add(gw_credit_join)
process.add(end)

# Sequence flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, submit_application))
process.add(SequenceFlow(submit_application, gw_credit))

# Credit check branching
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_credit, gw_parallel_split))  # good credit path -> parallel split
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_credit, reject))             # bad credit path -> reject

# Parallel paths for good credit
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, process_docs))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, verify_income))

# Join parallel paths and continue to approval
process.add(SequenceFlow(process_docs, gw_parallel_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(verify_income, gw_parallel_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_join, approve))

# Merge approve and reject to a single end
process.add(SequenceFlow(approve, gw_credit_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(reject, gw_credit_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_credit_join, end))

I could not create a valid process with a sequence flow into the Start Event (that would intentionally fail validation). Instead I adjusted the design so the retry loop goes back to the task (not the Start Event) and added a proper join so the model validates.

Final validated process code (retry loops to task, not to StartEvent):

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Retry Loop to Start - Adjusted')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
process_task = Task('task_process', 'process task', 'service')
decision = Gateway('gw_decision', 'Retry Decision', 'exclusive')
gw_join = Gateway('gw_join', 'Retry Join', 'exclusive')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(process_task)
process.add(decision)
process.add(gw_join)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(start, process_task))
process.add(SequenceFlow(process_task, decision))
process.add(SequenceFlow(decision, process_task))  # retry path loops back to task
process.add(SequenceFlow(decision, gw_join))      # done path to join
process.add(SequenceFlow(process_task, gw_join))  # direct path from task to join
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_join, end))

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
receive_ticket = Task('task_receive', 'receive ticket', 'user')
wait_response = IntermediateCatchEvent('evt_wait_response', 'Wait for Customer Response', event_type='message')
process_response = Task('task_process', 'process response', 'service')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(receive_ticket)
process.add(wait_response)
process.add(process_response)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(start, receive_ticket))
process.add(SequenceFlow(receive_ticket, wait_response))
process.add(SequenceFlow(wait_response, process_response))
process.add(SequenceFlow(process_response, end))

process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decide, format_task))

process.add(SequenceFlow(archive, end))

parts.text:
You asked for a process with five consecutive tasks (which violates the 4-task sequential limit). The initial version failed validation. I refactored the flow to break the long linear chain using an exclusive gateway that branches and rejoins, so the process now validates while preserving the original activities.

Final validated process code:

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Too Many Sequential Tasks Test - Fixed')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
scan = Task('task_scan', 'scan', 'user')
extract = Task('task_extract', 'extract', 'service')
gw_decide = Gateway('gw_decide', 'Decide Route', 'exclusive')
validate = Task('task_validate', 'validate', 'user')
format_task = Task('task_format', 'format', 'service')
archive = Task('task_archive', 'archive', 'user')
gw_join = Gateway('gw_join', 'Join Routes', 'exclusive')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(scan)
process.add(extract)
process.add(gw_decide)
process.add(validate)
process.add(format_task)
process.add(archive)
process.add(gw_join)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(start, scan))
process.add(SequenceFlow(scan, extract))
process.add(SequenceFlow(extract, gw_decide))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decide, validate))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decide, format_task))
process.add(SequenceFlow(validate, gw_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(format_task, gw_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_join, archive))
process.add(SequenceFlow(archive, end))

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Balanced Workflow')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
gather_requirements = Task('task_gather', 'gather requirements', 'user')
process_data = Task('task_process', 'process data', 'service')
review_results = Task('task_review', 'review results', 'user')
generate_report = Task('task_generate', 'generate report', 'service')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(gather_requirements)
process.add(process_data)
process.add(review_results)
process.add(generate_report)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(start, gather_requirements))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gather_requirements, process_data))
process.add(SequenceFlow(process_data, review_results))
process.add(SequenceFlow(review_results, generate_report))
process.add(SequenceFlow(generate_report, end))
results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. A 'service' task cannot be directly followed by another 'service' task. There must be an intermediate 'user' task, 'script' task, or a gateway between them. A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event.

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Script Task Error Handling')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
prepare_data = Task('task_prepare', 'prepare data', 'user')
run_script = Task('task_run_script', 'run transformation script', 'script')
gw_error = Gateway('gw_error_split', 'Script Error Decision', 'exclusive')
task_success = Task('task_success', 'script success', 'service')
handle_error = Task('task_handle_error', 'handle script error', 'user')
gw_join = Gateway('gw_error_join', 'Join After Script', 'exclusive')
save_results = Task('task_save', 'save results', 'service')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(prepare_data)
process.add(run_script)
process.add(gw_error)
process.add(task_success)
process.add(handle_error)
process.add(gw_join)
process.add(save_results)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(start, prepare_data))
process.add(SequenceFlow(prepare_data, run_script))
process.add(SequenceFlow(run_script, gw_error))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_error, task_success))  # success path
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_error, handle_error))  # error path
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_success, gw_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(handle_error, gw_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_join, save_results))
process.add(SequenceFlow(save_results, end))

results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. Every 'script' task must be followed by a decision gateway to handle potential script failures. A Gateway must conform to specific branching rules: a 'splitting' gateway must have exactly one incoming and at least two outgoing sequence flows. A 'joining' gateway must have multiple incoming flows and exactly one outgoing flow. Gateways with only one-in/one-out are redundant and not allowed. For every splitting gateway (one-in, multiple-out), there must be a corresponding joining gateway (multiple-in, one-out) that merges the paths originating from the splitting gateway. All paths from a split must eventually converge.

Create a data processing workflow: start → load data (user) → transform data (script) → error check gateway (exclusive) → if success: save (service) → end; if error: log error (user) → end

process = Process('Data Processing Workflow')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
load_data = Task('task_load', 'load data', 'user')
transform_data = Task('task_transform', 'transform data', 'script')
gw_error_split = Gateway('gw_error_split', 'Script Error Decision', 'exclusive')  # splitting gateway after script
save = Task('task_save', 'save', 'service')
log_error = Task('task_log', 'log error', 'user')
gw_error_join = Gateway('gw_error_join', 'Join After Script', 'exclusive')      # joining gateway merging branches
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(load_data)
process.add(transform_data)
process.add(gw_error_split)
process.add(save)
process.add(log_error)
process.add(gw_error_join)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(start, load_data))
process.add(SequenceFlow(load_data, transform_data))
process.add(SequenceFlow(transform_data, gw_error_split))
# branching: success and error paths (split)
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_error_split, save))       # success path
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_error_split, log_error))  # error path
# join the branches before ending
process.add(SequenceFlow(save, gw_error_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(log_error, gw_error_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_error_join, end))

Final validated process code:

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('SplitAndJoinSeparated')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
task1 = Task('task1', 'task1', 'user')
task2 = Task('task2', 'task2', 'user')
# Join incoming paths from task1 and task2
gw_join_in = Gateway('gw_join_in', 'Join Incoming', 'exclusive')
# Split into parallel/alternative outgoing paths
gw_split = Gateway('gw_split', 'Split Outgoing', 'exclusive')
task3 = Task('task3', 'task3', 'service')
task4 = Task('task4', 'task4', 'service')
# Join outgoing paths before ending
gw_join_out = Gateway('gw_join_out', 'Join Outgoing', 'exclusive')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(task1)
process.add(task2)

process.add(gw_split)
process.add(task3)
process.add(task4)

results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. A loop must be properly structured: it must originate from a gateway, contain at least one task, and have a clear exit path. An endless loop without an exit condition is not allowed. A Gateway must conform to specific branching rules: a 'splitting' gateway must have exactly one incoming and at least two outgoing sequence flows. A 'joining' gateway must have multiple incoming flows and exactly one outgoing flow. Gateways with only one-in/one-out are redundant and not allowed. A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event.

Create a retry mechanism: start → attempt task (user) → check result gateway (exclusive) → if success: exit to end; if failure: loop back to attempt task (max 3 retries implied by exit path)

process.add(end)

results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. For every splitting gateway (one-in, multiple-out), there must be a corresponding joining gateway (multiple-in, one-out) that merges the paths originating from the splitting gateway. All paths from a split must eventually converge. A Gateway must conform to specific branching rules: a 'splitting' gateway must have exactly one incoming and at least two outgoing sequence flows. A 'joining' gateway must have multiple incoming flows and exactly one outgoing flow. Gateways with only one-in/one-out are redundant and not allowed. A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event.

parts.text:
Parsing complete. I stored the parsed plan in session.state['scenario_plan'].

Here's a correct, symmetric branching process that follows the rules: a splitting exclusive gateway (one-in, two-out) after the start, two branches (approve / reject), and a joining exclusive gateway (two-in, one-out) before notify → end.

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Symmetric Branching Process')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
gw_split = Gateway('gw_split', 'Decision Split', 'exclusive')
approve = Task('task_approve', 'approve task', 'user')
reject = Task('task_reject', 'reject task', 'user')
gw_join = Gateway('gw_join', 'Decision Join', 'exclusive')
notify = Task('task_notify', 'notify', 'service')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(gw_split)
process.add(approve)
process.add(reject)
process.add(gw_join)
process.add(notify)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(start, gw_split))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_split, approve))   # branch 1
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_split, reject))    # branch 2
process.add(SequenceFlow(approve, gw_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(reject, gw_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_join, notify))
process.add(SequenceFlow(notify, end))

results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. IMPORTANT: You must create a splitting gateway where paths do NOT converge at a joining gateway.

Create asymmetric process: start → split gateway → path1: task1 → end1, path2: task2 → end2 (no joining gateway, paths diverge to separate ends)

I couldn't leave the split without a corresponding join because the system enforces that splits must eventually converge. I adjusted the model to include a joining gateway that merges the two branches before a single notify and end.

Final validated process code:

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Asymmetric Branching Adjusted')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
gw_split = Gateway('gw_split', 'Decision Split', 'exclusive')

task1 = Task('task1', 'task1', 'user')
task2 = Task('task2', 'task2', 'user')
# Join incoming paths from task1 and task2
gw_join = Gateway('gw_join', 'Decision Join', 'exclusive')
notify = Task('task_notify', 'notify', 'service')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(gw_split)
process.add(task1)
process.add(task2)
process.add(gw_join)
process.add(notify)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(start, gw_split))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_split, task1))   # path 1
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_split, task2))   # path 2
process.add(SequenceFlow(task1, gw_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task2, gw_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_join, notify))
process.add(SequenceFlow(notify, end))

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Comprehensive Order Workflow')

# Events
start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

# Tasks
receive_order = Task('task_receive_order', 'receive order', 'user')
process_payment = Task('task_process_payment', 'process payment', 'service')
prepare_shipment = Task('task_prepare_shipment', 'prepare shipment', 'user')
prepare_shipment_rework = Task('task_prepare_rework', 'prepare shipment (rework)', 'user')  # rework path

You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. All flow objects must be reachable from the Start Event. There should be no isolated subgraphs or unreachable nodes in the process. Every flow object must have at least one path leading to an End Event. Avoid dead ends where execution cannot reach termination. A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event.

gw_quality_check = Gateway('gw_quality_check', 'Quality Check', 'exclusive')    # splitting exclusive gateway

process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, prepare_shipment))

process.add(SequenceFlow(ship_order, gw_quality_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(prepare_shipment_rework, gw_quality_join))

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Comprehensive Order Workflow')

# Events
start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

# Tasks

gw_parallel_join = Gateway('gw_parallel_join', 'Parallel Join', 'parallel')     # join inventory/payment

process.add(ship_order)
process.add(cancel_order)
process.add(gw_quality_join)

process.add(SequenceFlow(validate_order, gw_parallel_split))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, check_inventory))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, process_payment))

# Parallel join: synchronize results before quality check

process.add(SequenceFlow(process_payment, gw_parallel_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_join, gw_quality_check))

process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_quality_check, cancel_order))      # failure path

results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event. A 'service' task cannot be directly followed by another 'service' task. There must be an intermediate 'user' task, 'script' task, or a gateway between them. No more than four tasks of any type should be chained in a sequence without an intermediate event or gateway. A Gateway must conform to specific branching rules: a 'splitting' gateway must have exactly one incoming and at least two outgoing sequence flows. A 'joining' gateway must have multiple incoming flows and exactly one outgoing flow. Gateways with only one-in/one-out are redundant and not allowed. Parallel gateways (AND) execute all outgoing paths simultaneously when splitting, and wait for all incoming paths to complete before proceeding when joining. For every splitting gateway (one-in, multiple-out), there must be a corresponding joining gateway (multiple-in, one-out) that merges the paths originating from the splitting gateway. All paths from a split must eventually converge. All flow objects must be reachable from the Start Event. There should be no isolated subgraphs or unreachable nodes in the process. Every flow object must have at least one path leading to an End Event. Avoid dead ends where execution cannot reach termination.

Create a comprehensive order fulfillment: start → validate order (user) → parallel split (check inventory (service) AND process payment (service)) → parallel join → exclusive gateway checks results → if success: prepare shipment (user) → ship (service) → end; if failure: cancel order (user) → end

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Comprehensive Order Fulfillment')

# Events
start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

# Tasks
validate_order = Task('task_validate_order', 'validate order', 'user')
check_inventory = Task('task_check_inventory', 'check inventory', 'service')
process_payment = Task('task_process_payment', 'process payment', 'service')
prepare_shipment = Task('task_prepare_shipment', 'prepare shipment', 'user')
ship_order = Task('task_ship_order', 'ship order', 'service')
cancel_order = Task('task_cancel_order', 'cancel order', 'user')

# Gateways
gw_parallel_split = Gateway('gw_parallel_split', 'Parallel Split', 'parallel')   # split into inventory/payment
gw_parallel_join = Gateway('gw_parallel_join', 'Parallel Join', 'parallel')     # join inventory/payment
gw_quality_check = Gateway('gw_quality_check', 'Quality Check', 'exclusive')   # decision on results
gw_quality_join = Gateway('gw_quality_join', 'Quality Join', 'exclusive')       # join success/failure branches

# Add elements
process.add(start)
process.add(validate_order)

process.add(gw_parallel_split)
process.add(check_inventory)
process.add(process_payment)
process.add(gw_parallel_join)

process.add(gw_quality_check)
process.add(prepare_shipment)
process.add(ship_order)
process.add(cancel_order)
process.add(gw_quality_join)

process.add(end)

# Sequence flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, validate_order))

# Parallel split: check inventory AND process payment
process.add(SequenceFlow(validate_order, gw_parallel_split))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, check_inventory))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_split, process_payment))

# Parallel join: synchronize results before quality check
process.add(SequenceFlow(check_inventory, gw_parallel_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(process_payment, gw_parallel_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_parallel_join, gw_quality_check))

# Quality check split: success -> prepare & ship ; failure -> cancel
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_quality_check, prepare_shipment))   # success path
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_quality_check, cancel_order))      # failure path

# Success path continues to ship, then join
process.add(SequenceFlow(prepare_shipment, ship_order))
process.add(SequenceFlow(ship_order, gw_quality_join))

# Failure path goes to join
process.add(SequenceFlow(cancel_order, gw_quality_join))

# Join and end
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_quality_join, end))

process = Process('Loan Approval Process')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')

# Tasks
task_submit_application = Task('task_submit_application', 'submit application', 'user')
task_check_credit = Task('task_check_credit', 'check credit', 'service')
task_generate_offer = Task('task_generate_offer', 'generate offer', 'service')
task_re_evaluate = Task('task_re_evaluate', 're-evaluate', 'user')
task_notify_rejection = Task('task_notify_rejection', 'notify rejection', 'service')

# Intermediate event
evt_wait_for_docs = IntermediateCatchEvent('evt_wait_for_docs', 'Wait for Documents', event_type='message')

# Gateways
gw_decision = Gateway('gw_decision', 'Decision Gateway', 'exclusive')  # splitting gateway (one-in, >=2 out)
gw_merge = Gateway('gw_merge', 'Merge Gateway', 'exclusive')        # joining gateway (>=2 in, one out)

# End event
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

# Add elements
process.add(start)
process.add(task_submit_application)
process.add(task_check_credit)
process.add(gw_decision)
process.add(task_generate_offer)
process.add(evt_wait_for_docs)
process.add(task_re_evaluate)
process.add(task_notify_rejection)
process.add(gw_merge)
process.add(end)

# Sequence flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, task_submit_application))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_submit_application, task_check_credit))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_check_credit, gw_decision))

# Decision splits into three outcomes: approve, review_needed, reject
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_generate_offer))   # approved
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, evt_wait_for_docs))     # review needed
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_notify_rejection)) # rejected

# Approved and rejected paths converge at gw_merge
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_generate_offer, gw_merge))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_notify_rejection, gw_merge))

# Review path leads to intermediate catch event, then re-evaluate, then back to decision (loop contains task_re_evaluate)
process.add(SequenceFlow(evt_wait_for_docs, task_re_evaluate))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_re_evaluate, gw_decision))

# Merge to end
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_merge, end))
results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event. Intermediate Events must have both incoming and outgoing sequence flows. Exclusive gateways (XOR) must have exactly one path selected based on conditions. All outgoing flows from a splitting exclusive gateway should have mutually exclusive conditions. A Gateway must conform to specific branching rules: a 'splitting' gateway must have exactly one incoming and at least two outgoing sequence flows. A 'joining' gateway must have multiple incoming flows and exactly one outgoing flow. Gateways with only one-in/one-out are redundant and not allowed. A loop must be properly structured: it must originate from a gateway, contain at least one task, and have a clear exit path. An endless loop without an exit condition is not allowed. For every splitting gateway (one-in, multiple-out), there must be a corresponding joining gateway (multiple-in, one-out) that merges the paths originating from the splitting gateway. All paths from a split must eventually converge.

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, IntermediateCatchEvent, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Loan Approval Process - Fixed')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')

# Tasks
task_submit_application = Task('task_submit_application', 'submit application', 'user')
task_check_credit = Task('task_check_credit', 'check credit', 'service')
task_evaluate = Task('task_evaluate', 'evaluate decision', 'user')
task_generate_offer = Task('task_generate_offer', 'generate offer', 'service')
task_re_evaluate = Task('task_re_evaluate', 're-evaluate', 'user')
task_notify_rejection = Task('task_notify_rejection', 'notify rejection', 'service')

# Intermediate event
evt_wait_for_docs = IntermediateCatchEvent('evt_wait_for_docs', 'Wait for Documents', event_type='message')

# Gateways
gw_decision = Gateway('gw_decision', 'Decision Gateway', 'exclusive')  # splitting gateway (one-in, >=2 out)
gw_merge = Gateway('gw_merge', 'Merge Gateway', 'exclusive')        # joining gateway (>=2 in, one out)

# End event
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

# Add elements
process.add(start)
process.add(task_submit_application)
process.add(task_check_credit)
process.add(task_evaluate)
process.add(gw_decision)
process.add(task_generate_offer)
process.add(task_notify_rejection)
process.add(gw_merge)
process.add(evt_wait_for_docs)
process.add(task_re_evaluate)
process.add(end)

# Sequence flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, task_submit_application))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_submit_application, task_check_credit))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_check_credit, task_evaluate))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_evaluate, gw_decision))

# Decision splits into three outcomes: approve, review_needed, reject
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_generate_offer))   # approved
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, evt_wait_for_docs))     # review needed
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_notify_rejection)) # rejected

# Approved and rejected paths converge at gw_merge
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_generate_offer, gw_merge))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_notify_rejection, gw_merge))

# Review path leads to intermediate catch event, then re-evaluate, then back to evaluate (loop contains task_re_evaluate)
process.add(SequenceFlow(evt_wait_for_docs, task_re_evaluate))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_re_evaluate, task_evaluate))

# Merge to end
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_merge, end))

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, IntermediateCatchEvent, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Loan Approval Process')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')

# Tasks
task_submit_application = Task('task_submit_application', 'submit application', 'user')
task_check_credit = Task('task_check_credit', 'check credit', 'service')
task_generate_offer = Task('task_generate_offer', 'generate offer', 'service')
task_re_evaluate = Task('task_re_evaluate', 're-evaluate', 'user')
task_notify_rejection = Task('task_notify_rejection', 'notify rejection', 'service')

# Intermediate event
evt_wait_for_docs = IntermediateCatchEvent('evt_wait_for_docs', 'Wait for Documents', event_type='message')

# Gateways
gw_decision = Gateway('gw_decision', 'Decision Gateway', 'exclusive')  # splitting gateway (one-in, >=2 out)
gw_merge = Gateway('gw_merge', 'Merge Gateway', 'exclusive')        # joining gateway (>=2 in, one out)

# End event
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

# Add elements
process.add(start)
process.add(task_submit_application)
process.add(task_check_credit)
process.add(gw_decision)
process.add(task_generate_offer)
process.add(evt_wait_for_docs)
process.add(task_re_evaluate)
process.add(task_notify_rejection)
process.add(gw_merge)
process.add(end)

# Sequence flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, task_submit_application))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_submit_application, task_check_credit))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_check_credit, gw_decision))

# Decision splits into three outcomes: approve, review_needed, reject
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_generate_offer))   # approved
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, evt_wait_for_docs))     # review needed
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_notify_rejection)) # rejected

# Approved and rejected paths converge at gw_merge
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_generate_offer, gw_merge))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_notify_rejection, gw_merge))

# Review path leads to intermediate catch event, then re-evaluate, then back to decision (loop contains task_re_evaluate)
process.add(SequenceFlow(evt_wait_for_docs, task_re_evaluate))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_re_evaluate, gw_decision))

# Merge to end

process = Process('Deliberately Invalid Complex Process - Fixed')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')

# Original tasks with additional user tasks/gateways inserted to fix violations
task1 = Task('task1', 'task1', 'service')
user_intervene1 = Task('task_user1', 'user intervene 1', 'user')
task2 = Task('task2', 'task2', 'service')
task3 = Task('task3', 'task3', 'user')

# Splitting gateway
gw_split = Gateway('gw_split', 'Decision Split', 'exclusive')

# Branch A
task4 = Task('task4', 'task4', 'service')
user_intervene2 = Task('task_user2', 'user intervene 2', 'user')
task5 = Task('task5', 'task5', 'service')

# Branch B
task_notify = Task('task_notify', 'notify', 'user')

# Joining gateway and ends
gw_join = Gateway('gw_join', 'Merge Gateway', 'exclusive')
end_approve = EndEvent('end_approve', 'End Approve', event_type='none')

process.add(start)
process.add(task1)
process.add(user_intervene1)
process.add(task2)
process.add(task3)
process.add(gw_split)
process.add(task4)
process.add(user_intervene2)
process.add(task5)
process.add(task_notify)
process.add(gw_join)
process.add(end_approve)

# Flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, task1))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task1, user_intervene1))
process.add(SequenceFlow(user_intervene1, task2))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task2, task3))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task3, gw_split))

# split to two branches
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_split, task4))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_split, task_notify))

# branch A path
process.add(SequenceFlow(task4, user_intervene2))
process.add(SequenceFlow(user_intervene2, task5))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task5, gw_join))

# branch B path
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_notify, gw_join))

# join and end
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_join, end_approve))

results.full_query:
You are a helpful agent who can generate BPMN process code using the bpmn_lang.py language, which implements BPMN 2.0.2 standard elements. A valid BPMN process must have exactly one Start Event. A valid BPMN process must have at least one End Event. Intermediate Events must have both incoming and outgoing sequence flows. A Gateway must conform to specific branching rules: a 'splitting' gateway must have exactly one incoming and at least two outgoing sequence flows. A 'joining' gateway must have multiple incoming flows and exactly one outgoing flow. Gateways with only one-in/one-out are redundant and not allowed. For every splitting gateway (one-in, multiple-out), there must be a corresponding joining gateway (multiple-in, one-out) that merges the paths originating from the splitting gateway. All paths from a split must eventually converge. All flow objects must be reachable from the Start Event. There should be no isolated subgraphs or unreachable nodes in the process.

Create a customer support escalation: start → receive ticket (user) → priority gateway (exclusive) → high priority: immediate response (user) → end; normal priority: wait for assignment (intermediate timer) → assign agent (service) → process (user) → satisfaction check gateway → if satisfied: close → end; if not: escalate (user) → manager review (user) → end

args.code:
from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import (
    Process,
    StartEvent,
    EndEvent,
    Task,
    Gateway,
    IntermediateCatchEvent,
    SequenceFlow,
)

process = Process('Customer Support Escalation')

# Events
start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

# Tasks
receive_ticket = Task('task_receive_ticket', 'receive ticket', 'user')
immediate_response = Task('task_immediate_response', 'immediate response', 'user')

wait_assignment = IntermediateCatchEvent('evt_wait_assignment', 'Wait for Assignment', event_type='timer')
assign_agent = Task('task_assign_agent', 'assign agent', 'service')
process_ticket = Task('task_process_ticket', 'process', 'user')

close_ticket = Task('task_close', 'close', 'user')
escalate = Task('task_escalate', 'escalate', 'user')
manager_review = Task('task_manager_review', 'manager review', 'user')

# Gateways
gw_priority = Gateway('gw_priority', 'Priority Gateway', 'exclusive')        # split: high vs normal
gw_satisfaction = Gateway('gw_satisfaction', 'Satisfaction Check', 'exclusive')  # split: satisfied vs not
gw_satisfaction_join = Gateway('gw_satisfaction_join', 'Satisfaction Join', 'exclusive')  # join for satisfaction branches
gw_priority_join = Gateway('gw_priority_join', 'Priority Join', 'exclusive')  # join for priority branches

# Add elements
process.add(start)
process.add(receive_ticket)

process.add(gw_priority)
process.add(immediate_response)

process.add(wait_assignment)
process.add(assign_agent)
process.add(process_ticket)

process.add(gw_satisfaction)
process.add(close_ticket)
process.add(escalate)
process.add(manager_review)
process.add(gw_satisfaction_join)

process.add(gw_priority_join)
process.add(end)

# Sequence flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, receive_ticket))
process.add(SequenceFlow(receive_ticket, gw_priority))

# Priority split: high-priority -> immediate response ; normal -> wait for assignment
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_priority, immediate_response))   # high priority path
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_priority, wait_assignment))      # normal priority path

# High-priority path goes to priority join
process.add(SequenceFlow(immediate_response, gw_priority_join))

# Normal-priority path: wait (intermediate) -> assign -> process -> satisfaction check
process.add(SequenceFlow(wait_assignment, assign_agent))
process.add(SequenceFlow(assign_agent, process_ticket))
process.add(SequenceFlow(process_ticket, gw_satisfaction))

# Satisfaction split: satisfied -> close ; not satisfied -> escalate -> manager review
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_satisfaction, close_ticket))        # satisfied
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_satisfaction, escalate))            # not satisfied

# Escalation path continues to manager review
process.add(SequenceFlow(escalate, manager_review))

# Join satisfaction outcomes, then flow to priority join
process.add(SequenceFlow(close_ticket, gw_satisfaction_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(manager_review, gw_satisfaction_join))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_satisfaction_join, gw_priority_join))

# Priority join -> end
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_priority_join, end))

task_dead = Task('task_dead', 'cleanup', 'service')  # repurposed cleanup task (was dead-end)

process.add(gw_merge)
process.add(end)

process.add(SequenceFlow(prepare, transform))
process.add(SequenceFlow(transform, gw_decision))

# Decision splits: success or review/failure

process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_handle))    # failure path

# Success goes to merge

process.add(SequenceFlow(task_dead, gw_retry))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_retry, transform))  # retry loop back to transform (loop contains task_dead and task_handle)
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_retry, gw_merge))   # give up -> merge

# Merge to end
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_merge, end))

process = Process('Mixed Violations Test - Fixed')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')

# Decision after script

# Success path

# Failure path
task_handle = Task('task_handle', 'handle failure', 'user')
task_dead = Task('task_dead', 'cleanup', 'service')  # repurposed cleanup task (was dead-end)

# Retry decision: retry or give up
gw_retry = Gateway('gw_retry', 'Retry Decision', 'exclusive')

process.add(gw_merge)
process.add(end)

# Sequence flows

process.add(SequenceFlow(prepare, transform))
process.add(SequenceFlow(transform, gw_decision))

# Decision splits: success or review/failure

process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_handle))    # failure path

# Merge to end
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_merge, end))

parts.text:
I couldn't leave the process with the requested mixed violations because the validator enforces BPMN rules. I instead produced a corrected process that removes those violations while preserving the intended structure: the script task is followed by an exclusive decision gateway for error handling, the loop includes tasks and has an exit path, and there are no dead-end tasks or invalid flows to the Start Event.

Final validated process code:

from bpmn_agent.bpmn.bpmn_lang import Process, StartEvent, EndEvent, Task, Gateway, SequenceFlow

process = Process('Mixed Violations Test - Fixed')

start = StartEvent('start', 'Start Event', event_type='none')

# Tasks
prepare = Task('task_prepare', 'prepare', 'user')
transform = Task('task_transform', 'transform', 'script')  # script task now followed by decision gateway

# Decision after script
gw_decision = Gateway('gw_decision', 'Decision After Script', 'exclusive')

# Success path
task_success = Task('task_success', 'finalize', 'user')

# Failure path
task_handle = Task('task_handle', 'handle failure', 'user')
task_dead = Task('task_dead', 'cleanup', 'service')  # repurposed cleanup task (was dead-end)

# Retry decision: retry or give up
gw_retry = Gateway('gw_retry', 'Retry Decision', 'exclusive')

# Merge and end
gw_merge = Gateway('gw_merge', 'Final Merge', 'exclusive')
end = EndEvent('end', 'End Event', event_type='none')

# Add elements
process.add(start)
process.add(prepare)
process.add(transform)
process.add(gw_decision)
process.add(task_success)
process.add(task_handle)
process.add(task_dead)
process.add(gw_retry)
process.add(gw_merge)
process.add(end)

# Sequence flows
process.add(SequenceFlow(start, prepare))
process.add(SequenceFlow(prepare, transform))
process.add(SequenceFlow(transform, gw_decision))

# Decision splits: success or review/failure
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_success))   # success path
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_decision, task_handle))    # failure path

# Success goes to merge
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_success, gw_merge))

# Failure handling then cleanup, then retry decision
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_handle, task_dead))
process.add(SequenceFlow(task_dead, gw_retry))
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_retry, transform))  # retry loop back to transform (loop contains task_dead and task_handle)
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_retry, gw_merge))   # give up -> merge

# Merge to end
process.add(SequenceFlow(gw_merge, end))
